Analiza_sentymentu_w_czasie_dlugosc_linii.R

zuzannarafal

2026-03-17

# Analiza sentymentu w czasie ----

library(SentimentAnalysis)
## 
## Attaching package: 'SentimentAnalysis'
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     write
library(ggplot2)
library(ggthemes)
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
# Jeden cały plik ----

# Wczytanie danych tekstowych
# Odczytanie lokalnego pliku .txt
text <- readLines("Trump - State of the Union_25.02.2026.txt", encoding="UTF-8")



# Usunięcie pustych wierszy
non_empty_lines <- text[nzchar(text)]


# Połączenie wszystkich wierszy w jeden ciąg znaków
full_text <- paste(non_empty_lines, collapse = " ")

# Usunięcie zbędnych spacji
full_text <- gsub("\\s+", " ", full_text)

# Funkcja do dzielenia tekstu na segmenty o określonej długości
split_text_into_chunks <- function(text, chunk_size) {
  start_positions <- seq(1, nchar(text), by = chunk_size)
  chunks <- substring(text, start_positions, start_positions + chunk_size - 1)
  return(chunks)
}

#install.packages("SnowballC")

# Podzielenie tekstu na segmenty
#
# ustaw min_lentgh jako jednolitą długość jednego segmentu
min_length <- 50
text_chunks <- split_text_into_chunks(full_text, min_length)


# Wyświetlenie wynikowych segmentów
print(text_chunks)
##    [1] "Speaker Johnson, Vice President Vance, first lady "
##    [2] "of the United States, second lady of the United St"
##    [3] "ates, members of Congress and my fellow Americans,"
##    [4] " our nation is back: Bigger, better, richer and st"
##    [5] "ronger than ever before. Less than five months fro"
##    [6] "m now, our country will celebrate an epic mileston"
##    [7] "e in American history, the 250th anniversary of ou"
##    [8] "r glorious American independence. This July 4th, w"
##    [9] "e will mark two and a half centuries of liberty an"
##   [10] "d triumph, progress and freedom in the most incred"
##   [11] "ible and exceptional nation ever to exist on the f"
##   [12] "ace of the earth. And we’ve seen nothing yet. We’r"
##   [13] "e going to do better and better and better. This i"
##   [14] "s the golden age of America. When I spoke in this "
##   [15] "chamber 12 months ago, I had just inherited a nati"
##   [16] "on in crisis, with a stagnant economy, inflation a"
##   [17] "t record levels, a wide-open border, horrendous re"
##   [18] "cruitment for military and police, rampant crime a"
##   [19] "t home and wars and chaos all over the world. But "
##   [20] "tonight, after just one year, I can say with digni"
##   [21] "ty and pride that we have achieved a transformatio"
##   [22] "n like no one has ever seen before and a turnaroun"
##   [23] "d for the ages. It is, indeed, a turnaround for th"
##   [24] "e ages. And we will never go back to where we were"
##   [25] " just a very short time ago. We’re not going back."
##   [26] " Today our border is secure. Today our border is s"
##   [27] "ecure, our spirit is restored, inflation is plumme"
##   [28] "ting, incomes are rising fast, the roaring economy"
##   [29] " is roaring like never before, our enemies are sca"
##   [30] "red, our military and police are stacked, and Amer"
##   [31] "ica is respected again, perhaps like never before."
##   [32] " After four years in which millions and millions o"
##   [33] "f illegal aliens poured across our borders totally"
##   [34] " unfettered and unchecked, we now have the stronge"
##   [35] "st and most secure border in American history, by "
##   [36] "far. In the past nine months, zero illegal aliens "
##   [37] "have been admitted to the United States. But we wi"
##   [38] "ll always allow people to come in legally, people "
##   [39] "that will love our country and will work hard to m"
##   [40] "aintain our country. Deadly fentanyl across our bo"
##   [41] "rder is down by a record 56% in one year. In the l"
##   [42] "ast year, the murder rate saw its single largest d"
##   [43] "ecline in recorded history. This is the biggest de"
##   [44] "cline, think of it, in recorded history, the lowes"
##   [45] "t number in over 125 years, since 1900. In fact, s"
##   [46] "ubstantially before my wonderful father — I had a "
##   [47] "wonderful father, Fred — before he was born, subst"
##   [48] "antially before he was born. That’s a long time ag"
##   [49] "o, he wouldn’t like me to say that, but that’s a l"
##   [50] "ong time ago. The Biden administration and its all"
##   [51] "ies in Congress gave us the worst inflation in the"
##   [52] " history of our country. But in 12 months, my admi"
##   [53] "nistration has driven core inflation down to the l"
##   [54] "owest level in more than five years. And in the la"
##   [55] "st three months of 2025, it was down to 1.7%. Gaso"
##   [56] "line, which reached a peak of over $6 a gallon in "
##   [57] "some states under my predecessor and was, quite ho"
##   [58] "nestly, a disaster, is now below $2.30 a gallon in"
##   [59] " most states, and in some places $1.99 a gallon. A"
##   [60] "nd when I visited the great state of Iowa just a f"
##   [61] "ew weeks ago, I even saw $1.85 a gallon for gasoli"
##   [62] "ne, the lowest in four years, and falling fast. An"
##   [63] "d the annual cost of a typical new mortgage is dow"
##   [64] "n almost $5,000 just since I took office, one year"
##   [65] ". And lower interest rates will solve the Biden-cr"
##   [66] "eated housing problem, while at the same time prot"
##   [67] "ecting the values of those people who already own "
##   [68] "a house that really feel rich for the first time i"
##   [69] "n their lives. We want to protect those values. We"
##   [70] " want to keep those values up. We’re going to do b"
##   [71] "oth — and keep it that way. The stock market has s"
##   [72] "et 53 all-time record highs since the election. Th"
##   [73] "ink of that, one year, boosting pensions, 401(k)s "
##   [74] "and retirement accounts for the millions and the m"
##   [75] "illions of Americans, they’re all gaining. Everybo"
##   [76] "dy is up, way up. In four long years, the last adm"
##   [77] "inistration got less than $1 trillion in new inves"
##   [78] "tment in the United States. And when I say less, s"
##   [79] "ubstantially less. In 12 months, I secured commitm"
##   [80] "ents for more than $18 trillion pouring in from al"
##   [81] "l over the globe. Think of it — much less than $1 "
##   [82] "trillion for four years, versus much more than $18"
##   [83] " trillion for one year. What a difference a presid"
##   [84] "ent makes. A short time ago, we were a dead countr"
##   [85] "y. Now we are the hottest country anywhere in the "
##   [86] "world. The hottest. As thousands of new businesses"
##   [87] " are forming and factories, plants and laboratorie"
##   [88] "s are being built, we have added 70,000 new constr"
##   [89] "uction jobs in just a very short period of time. G"
##   [90] "etting bigger and bigger and stronger. Nobody can "
##   [91] "believe what they’re watching. American oil produc"
##   [92] "tion is up by more than 600,000 barrels a day. And"
##   [93] " we just received from our new friend and partner,"
##   [94] " Venezuela, more than 80 million barrels of oil. A"
##   [95] "merican natural gas production is at an all-time h"
##   [96] "igh. Because I kept my promise to drill, baby, dri"
##   [97] "ll. More Americans are working today than at any t"
##   [98] "ime in the history of our country. Think about tha"
##   [99] "t. Any time in the history of our country. More wo"
##  [100] "rking today, and 100% of all jobs created under my"
##  [101] " administration have been in the private sector. W"
##  [102] "e ended DEI in America. We cut a record number of "
##  [103] "job-killing regulations, and in one year we have l"
##  [104] "ifted 2.4 million Americans - a record - off of fo"
##  [105] "od stamps. And for all of these reasons, I say ton"
##  [106] "ight, members of Congress, the state of our Union "
##  [107] "is strong. Our country is winning again. In fact, "
##  [108] "we’re winning so much that we really don’t know wh"
##  [109] "at to do about it. People are asking me, please, p"
##  [110] "lease, please, Mr. President, we’re winning too mu"
##  [111] "ch. We can’t take it anymore. We’re not used to wi"
##  [112] "nning in our country until you came along, we’re j"
##  [113] "ust always losing. But now we’re winning too much."
##  [114] " And I say, no, no, no, you’re going to win again."
##  [115] " You’re going to win big. You’re going to win bigg"
##  [116] "er than ever. And to prove that point, to prove th"
##  [117] "at point, here with us tonight is a group of winne"
##  [118] "rs who just made the entire nation proud. The men’"
##  [119] "s gold medal Olympic hockey team. Come on in. Go a"
##  [120] "head. It’s the first time I’ve ever seen them get "
##  [121] "up. And actually, not all of them did get up. But "
##  [122] "they beat a fantastic Canadian team in overtime, a"
##  [123] "s everybody saw, as did the American women who wil"
##  [124] "l soon be coming to the White House. They were in "
##  [125] "the Oval Office before, and I just want to say a s"
##  [126] "econd, very big congratulations to Team USA. But I"
##  [127] " have to say that - and I told them this and we to"
##  [128] "ok a vote of the team. I said, anybody votes no, I"
##  [129] "’m not doing it. So they stood there. They weren’t"
##  [130] " about to say no because I’ve never seen a goalten"
##  [131] "der play as well as goalie Connor Hellebuyck. Thin"
##  [132] "k of it, 46 shots on goal. And I asked him - one s"
##  [133] "hot, the one where you put your stick in the back "
##  [134] "and it hit the neck of your stick and bounced off "
##  [135] "- you practice that? Or was that a little lucky? H"
##  [136] "e refused to answer that question. But I just want"
##  [137] " to tell you that the members of this great hockey"
##  [138] " squad will be very happy to hear, based on their "
##  [139] "vote and my vote. And in this case, my vote was mo"
##  [140] "re important, that I will soon be presenting Conno"
##  [141] "r with our highest civilian honor, which we will b"
##  [142] "e given and which has been given to many athletes "
##  [143] "over the years. But when I say many, not too many,"
##  [144] " like 12, it’s called the highest civilian honor i"
##  [145] "n our country. The Presidential Medal of Freedom. "
##  [146] "Great athletes have gotten that. Very great, the b"
##  [147] "est. And I thought he deserved it. And I did take "
##  [148] "a vote. Every single one of them. I said, I’m not "
##  [149] "giving it if anybody goes no, and every single one"
##  [150] " of them rapidly put up their hand. So I want to t"
##  [151] "hank you all. What a special job you did. What spe"
##  [152] "cial champions you are. Thank you very much. I’m a"
##  [153] "lso pleased to say that the next time the Olympic "
##  [154] "torch is lit, it will be here in America for the 2"
##  [155] "028 Olympics. And it’s the summer version, right? "
##  [156] "In Los Angeles. We’re going to do a good job in Lo"
##  [157] "s Angeles. And Los Angeles is going to be safe, ju"
##  [158] "st like Washington, D.C. is now one of the safest "
##  [159] "cities in the country. And this year, and I must s"
##  [160] "ay, I got them both. I got them in the first term."
##  [161] " And I was disappointed because I didn’t think I’d"
##  [162] " be the president when this happened. But strange "
##  [163] "things took place, and now I’ve got them because I"
##  [164] " got the Olympics. And I’ve got the 2026 FIFA Worl"
##  [165] "d Cup, and I wanted to claim the 250th, but I didn"
##  [166] "’t get away with that one. I couldn’t claim that o"
##  [167] "ne for myself. But we’re getting the World Cup, so"
##  [168] " we have the World Cup and the Olympics coming and"
##  [169] " that is exciting news. So this will be a year to "
##  [170] "celebrate our country and the heroes who have kept"
##  [171] " it free. Men like Buddy Taggart. At age 17, Buddy"
##  [172] " volunteered to defend America in World War II, se"
##  [173] "rving in the Pacific under the great General Dougl"
##  [174] "as MacArthur. He fought bravely in the famous Batt"
##  [175] "le of Manila. He worked so hard he was badly wound"
##  [176] "ed and almost killed by enemy machine guns at Luzo"
##  [177] "n. And 81 years ago this month, he liberated the l"
##  [178] "argest internment camp in the Philippines, one of "
##  [179] "the largest anywhere in the world. But he earned m"
##  [180] "any honors, including a Purple Heart and a Bronze "
##  [181] "Star, came home, started a family, and tonight he "
##  [182] "is in the gallery, looking forward to July 4th, 20"
##  [183] "26, his 100th birthday. So Buddy, you’re a brave m"
##  [184] "an and we salute you. Even in times of challenge, "
##  [185] "setback and immense heartache, the spirit of 1776 "
##  [186] "has always shone through very brightly. It was Jul"
##  [187] "y 4th of last year when floodwaters tore through a"
##  [188] " girls summer camp in central Texas. One of the wo"
##  [189] "rst things I’ve ever seen. I was there, rising 26 "
##  [190] "feet in a matter of minutes, tragically claiming m"
##  [191] "any, many lives. You all remember that one. As the"
##  [192] " waters threatened to sweep her away, 11-year-old "
##  [193] "Milly Cate McClymond closed her eyes and prayed to"
##  [194] " God. She thought she was going to die. Those pray"
##  [195] "ers were answered when Coast Guard rescue swimmer "
##  [196] "Scott Ruskan descended from a helicopter above. No"
##  [197] "body knew where he came from. It was Scott’s first"
##  [198] " ever rescue mission — young guy, but very brave, "
##  [199] "very, very top. Always top in his class. And he li"
##  [200] "fted not just Milly Cate, but 164 others to safety"
##  [201] ". People watched Scott from a distance and they co"
##  [202] "uldn’t believe what they were seeing. The winds we"
##  [203] "re blowing, the rain was pouring, everything was g"
##  [204] "oing. And that rapid water, nobody’s ever seen any"
##  [205] "thing like it. They said, wow, that’s something. T"
##  [206] "onight, Scott and Milly Cate are here together, re"
##  [207] "united for the very first time. Thank you, Scott, "
##  [208] "Milly Cate. And Petty Officer Ruskan, I’m pleased "
##  [209] "to inform you that I am now awarding you the Legio"
##  [210] "n of Merit for extraordinary heroism. Which is wha"
##  [211] "t it was, extraordinary heroism. Thank you. And I’"
##  [212] "d like to have the military aide to please come do"
##  [213] "wn and take care of this service. Military aide, t"
##  [214] "hank you very much. Take care of that very importa"
##  [215] "nt service. Thank you very much. From 1776 to toda"
##  [216] "y, every generation of Americans has step forward "
##  [217] "to defend life, liberty and the pursuit of happine"
##  [218] "ss. And they were doing it for the next generation"
##  [219] ". But now it’s our turn. Together we’re building a"
##  [220] " nation where every child has the chance to reach "
##  [221] "higher and go further. Where government answers to"
##  [222] " the people, not the powerful, and where the inter"
##  [223] "ests of hardworking American citizens are always o"
##  [224] "ur first and ultimate concern. That is the debt we"
##  [225] " owe to the heroes who came before us. And that is"
##  [226] " the promise we must keep to America for our 250th"
##  [227] " year. Last year, I urged this Congress to begin t"
##  [228] "he mission by passing the largest tax cuts in Amer"
##  [229] "ican history, and our Republican majorities delive"
##  [230] "red so beautifully. Thank you Republicans. All Dem"
##  [231] "ocrats, every single one of them, voted against th"
##  [232] "ese really important and very necessary massive ta"
##  [233] "x cuts. They wanted large-scale tax increases to h"
##  [234] "urt the people, instead. But we held strong. And w"
##  [235] "ith the great Big Beautiful Bill, we gave you no t"
##  [236] "ax on tips, no tax on overtime and no tax on Socia"
##  [237] "l Security for our great seniors. And we also made"
##  [238] " interest on auto loans tax deductible. The first "
##  [239] "time, but only if the car is made in America. Rece"
##  [240] "ntly in Pennsylvania, I met Megan Hemhauser, a dev"
##  [241] "oted mom who homeschools her children. Beautiful t"
##  [242] "wo children during the day, while waiting tables a"
##  [243] "t night as her husband works overtime operating ve"
##  [244] "ry heavy equipment. Megan is here this evening and"
##  [245] " she’s happy to tell you that she is so, so much r"
##  [246] "icher because with no tax on tips, no tax on overt"
##  [247] "ime, and our expanded child tax credit done again "
##  [248] "by Republicans, Megan and her husband will take ho"
##  [249] "me more than $5,000 extra just for the year, cutti"
##  [250] "ng her tax bill in more than half. Megan, please s"
##  [251] "tand up. We’re fighting for you, Megan. Thank you,"
##  [252] " Megan. Thank you, Megan, we’re fighting for you M"
##  [253] "egan. Under our bill, parents like Megan can also "
##  [254] "activate their children’s brand new Trump Accounts"
##  [255] ". And I didn’t name it. I did not. I did not name "
##  [256] "them. Nobody believes me, but I did not name it. I"
##  [257] "t was named by a very tall man standing right ther"
##  [258] "e in the third row. Nice man, a good man. Tax free"
##  [259] " investment accounts for every American child. Thi"
##  [260] "s is something that’s so special. Has taken off an"
##  [261] "d gone through the roof. Millions will be refunded"
##  [262] " courtesy of the U.S. Treasury and private individ"
##  [263] "uals like Michael and Susan Dell, who have donated"
##  [264] " 6 billion, 250 million dollars to fund the Trump "
##  [265] "accounts for 25 million American children. They’re"
##  [266] " great people. When I asked Michael Dell, “How do "
##  [267] "you make all that money?” He said, “Well, I just s"
##  [268] "at in my dorm in school, and I made computers and "
##  [269] "I’d sell them to people. And I just kept selling a"
##  [270] "nd selling and selling and ...” Pretty amazing sto"
##  [271] "ry. That’s called the American Dream. He sold a lo"
##  [272] "t of computers, a lot of those laptops. So I congr"
##  [273] "atulate him on that. But I really thank him and Su"
##  [274] "san, as well as others like Brad Gerstner, a very "
##  [275] "tremendous guy. He was behind it right from the be"
##  [276] "ginning. Brad Gerstner. Thank you, Brad. So with m"
##  [277] "odest additional contributions, these young people"
##  [278] "’s accounts could grow to over $100,000 or more by"
##  [279] " the time they turn 18. Think of it. How much mone"
##  [280] "y is that for somebody that started with nothing, "
##  [281] "over $100,000, could be much more than that. To ma"
##  [282] "ke this investment in our children’s future, go to"
##  [283] " trumpaccounts.gov, trumpaccounts.gov. And a lot o"
##  [284] "f people are doing it. They’re setting every recor"
##  [285] "d in the book. It’s popular. They knew what was go"
##  [286] "ing to, what it was going to take. So proud of the"
##  [287] "m. I’m so proud of the people that got it started."
##  [288] " And now it’s just taken off. One of the primary r"
##  [289] "easons for our country’s stunning economic turnaro"
##  [290] "und, the biggest in history — where the Dow Jones "
##  [291] "broke 50,000 four years ahead of schedule, and the"
##  [292] " S&P hit 7,000, where it wasn’t supposed to do it "
##  [293] "for many years — were tariffs. I used these tariff"
##  [294] "s, took in hundreds of billions of dollars to make"
##  [295] " great deals for our country, both economically an"
##  [296] "d on a national security basis. Everything was wor"
##  [297] "king well. Countries that were ripping us off for "
##  [298] "decades are now paying us hundreds of billions of "
##  [299] "dollars. They were ripping us so badly. You all kn"
##  [300] "ow that? Everybody knows it. Even the Democrats kn"
##  [301] "ow it. They just don’t want to say it. And yet the"
##  [302] "se countries are now happy and so are we. We made "
##  [303] "deals -- the deals are all done and they’re happy."
##  [304] " They’re not making money like they used to. But w"
##  [305] "e’re making a lot of money. There was no inflation"
##  [306] ", tremendous growth. And the big story was how Don"
##  [307] "ald Trump called the economy correctly and 22 Nobe"
##  [308] "l Prize winners in economics didn’t. They got it t"
##  [309] "otally wrong. They got it really wrong. And then j"
##  [310] "ust four days ago, an unfortunate ruling from the "
##  [311] "United States Supreme Court, it just came down, ca"
##  [312] "me down. Very unfortunate ruling. But the good new"
##  [313] "s is that almost all countries and corporations wa"
##  [314] "nt to keep the deal that they already made — right"
##  [315] ", Scott? — knowing that the legal power that I, as"
##  [316] " president, have to make a new deal could be far w"
##  [317] "orse for them, and, therefore, they will continue "
##  [318] "to work along the same successful path that we had"
##  [319] " negotiated before the Supreme Court’s unfortunate"
##  [320] " involvement. So, despite the disappointing ruling"
##  [321] ", these powerful countries saving, is saving our c"
##  [322] "ountry the kind of money we’re taking in, peace pr"
##  [323] "otecting — many of the wars I’ve settled was becau"
##  [324] "se of the threat of tariffs, I wouldn’t have been "
##  [325] "able to settle them without — will remain in place"
##  [326] " under fully approved and tested alternative legal"
##  [327] " statutes. And they have been tested for a long ti"
##  [328] "me. They’re a little more complex, but they’re act"
##  [329] "ually probably better — leading to a solution that"
##  [330] " will be even stronger than before. Congressional "
##  [331] "action will not be necessary. It’s already time-te"
##  [332] "sted and approved. And as time goes by, I believe "
##  [333] "the tariffs, paid for by foreign countries, will, "
##  [334] "like in the past, substantially replace the modern"
##  [335] "-day system of income tax, taking a great financia"
##  [336] "l burden off the people that I love. Moving forwar"
##  [337] "d, factories, jobs, investment and trillions and t"
##  [338] "rillions of dollars will continue pouring into the"
##  [339] " United States of America because we finally have "
##  [340] "a president who puts America first. I put America "
##  [341] "first. I love America. For decades before I came a"
##  [342] "long, we had the exact opposite. From trade to hea"
##  [343] "lth care, from energy to immigration, everything w"
##  [344] "as stolen and rigged in order to drain the wealth "
##  [345] "out of the productive, hardworking people who make"
##  [346] " our country great, who make our country run. Unde"
##  [347] "r Biden and his corrupt partners in Congress and b"
##  [348] "eyond, it reached a breaking point with the Green "
##  [349] "New Scam, open borders for everyone — they poured "
##  [350] "in by the millions and millions from prisons, from"
##  [351] " mental institutions, they were murderers — 11,888"
##  [352] " murders — they came into our country, you allowed"
##  [353] " that to happen. And record-setting inflation that"
##  [354] " cost the typical family $34,000 in just a speck o"
##  [355] "f time. Now, the same people in this chamber who v"
##  [356] "oted for those disasters suddenly used the word af"
##  [357] "fordability, a word, they just used it because som"
##  [358] "ebody gave it to them, knowing full well that they"
##  [359] " caused and created the increased prices that all "
##  [360] "of our citizens had to endure. You caused that pro"
##  [361] "blem. You caused that problem. They knew their sta"
##  [362] "tements were a lie, they knew it, they knew their "
##  [363] "statements were a dirty, rotten lie. Their policie"
##  [364] "s created the high prices. Our policies are rapidl"
##  [365] "y ending them. We are doing really well. Those pri"
##  [366] "ces are plummeting downward. The price of eggs is "
##  [367] "down 60%, Madam Secretary, thank you. The cost of "
##  [368] "chicken, butter, fruit, hotels, automobiles, rent "
##  [369] "is lower today than when I took office by a lot. A"
##  [370] "nd even beef, which was very high, is starting to "
##  [371] "come down significantly. Just hold on a little whi"
##  [372] "le. We’ll get that down. And soon you will see num"
##  [373] "bers that few people would think were possible to "
##  [374] "achieve just a short time ago. Nobody can believe "
##  [375] "when they see the kind of numbers, and especially "
##  [376] "energy, when they see energy going down to numbers"
##  [377] " like that, they cannot believe it. It’s like anot"
##  [378] "her big tax cut. I’m also confronting one of the b"
##  [379] "iggest rip-offs of our times, the crushing cost of"
##  [380] " health care, caused by you. Since the passage of "
##  [381] "the “Unaffordable Care Act,” sometimes referred to"
##  [382] " as Obamacare, big insurance companies have gotten"
##  [383] " rich. It was meant for the insurance companies, n"
##  [384] "ot for the people. With our government giving them"
##  [385] " hundreds and hundreds of billions of dollars a ye"
##  [386] "ar, as their stock prices soared 1,000, 1,200, 1,4"
##  [387] "00 and even 1,700%, like nothing else. That’s why "
##  [388] "I introduced the great health care plan. I want to"
##  [389] " stop all payments to big insurance companies and "
##  [390] "instead give that money directly to the people, so"
##  [391] " they can buy their own health care, which will be"
##  [392] " better health care at a much lower cost. In addit"
##  [393] "ion, my plan requires maximum price transparency. "
##  [394] "That’s a big deal. Sounds so simple, so big. And I"
##  [395] " did that in my first term and the Democrats immed"
##  [396] "iately terminated it, with the full knowledge that"
##  [397] " they were doing a very bad thing for the people. "
##  [398] "Costs were going to go way up and that’s what happ"
##  [399] "ened, and now I’m bringing them way down on health"
##  [400] " care and everything else. I’m also ending the wil"
##  [401] "dly inflated cost of prescription drugs like has n"
##  [402] "ever happened before. Other presidents tried to do"
##  [403] " it, but they never could. They tried, most didn’t"
##  [404] " try, actually, but they tried, they said they tri"
##  [405] "ed. They couldn’t do it. They didn’t even come clo"
##  [406] "se. They were all talk and no action. But I got it"
##  [407] " done, under my just-enacted most-favored nation a"
##  [408] "greements, Americans who have for decades paid by "
##  [409] "far the highest prices of any nation anywhere in t"
##  [410] "he world for prescription drugs will now pay the l"
##  [411] "owest price anywhere in the world for drugs, anywh"
##  [412] "ere, the lowest price. So, in my first year of my "
##  [413] "second term — should be my third term, but strange"
##  [414] " things happen — I took prescription drugs. a very"
##  [415] " big part of health care, from the highest price i"
##  [416] "n the entire world to the lowest. That’s a big ach"
##  [417] "ievement. The result is price differences of 300, "
##  [418] "400, 500, 600% and more, all available right now a"
##  [419] "t a new website called trumprx.gov — and I didn’t "
##  [420] "name that one, either, by the way. And here tonigh"
##  [421] "t is the very first customer ever to get that big "
##  [422] "discount, and it is big, Catherine Rayner. For fiv"
##  [423] "e years, she and her husband have struggled with i"
##  [424] "nfertility and they turned to IVF. One drug has be"
##  [425] "en costing Catherine $4,000 to purchase, but a few"
##  [426] " weeks ago she logged onto the TrumpRX website and"
##  [427] " got that same drug that cost $4,000, got it for u"
##  [428] "nder $500, a reduction of much more, actually, tha"
##  [429] "n $3,500. Catherine, we are all praying for you an"
##  [430] "d you’re going to be a great mom. So now I’m calli"
##  [431] "ng on Congress to codify my most-favored-nation pr"
##  [432] "ogram into law. Now, the one thing I’m not sure it"
##  [433] " matters, because it’s going to be very hard for s"
##  [434] "omebody that comes along after me to say, let’s ra"
##  [435] "ise drug prices by 700 or 800%. But, John and Mike"
##  [436] ", if you don’t mind, codify it anyway, they may do"
##  [437] " it. Codify it anyway. Thank you. And many America"
##  [438] "ns are also concerned that energy demand from AI d"
##  [439] "ata centers could unfairly drive up their electric"
##  [440] " utility bills. Tonight, I’m pleased to announce t"
##  [441] "hat I have negotiated the new ratepayer protection"
##  [442] " pledge. You know what that is? We’re telling the "
##  [443] "major tech companies that they have the obligation"
##  [444] " to provide for their own power needs. They can bu"
##  [445] "ild their own power plants as part of their factor"
##  [446] "y, so that no one’s prices will go up and in many "
##  [447] "cases, prices of electricity will go down for the "
##  [448] "community, and very substantially then. This is a "
##  [449] "unique strategy never used in this country before."
##  [450] " We have an old grid. It could never handle the ki"
##  [451] "nd of numbers, the amount of electricity that’s ne"
##  [452] "eded. So I’m telling them they can build their own"
##  [453] " plant, they’re going to produce their own electri"
##  [454] "city. It will ensure the company’s ability to get "
##  [455] "electricity, while at the same time lowering price"
##  [456] "s of electricity for you and could be very substan"
##  [457] "tial. For all of these cities and towns, you’re go"
##  [458] "ing to see some good things happen over the next n"
##  [459] "umber of years. Another pillar of the American dre"
##  [460] "am that has been under attack has been home owners"
##  [461] "hip. With us tonight is Rachel Wiggins, a mom of t"
##  [462] "wo from Houston. She placed bids on 20 homes and l"
##  [463] "ost all of those bids to gigantic investment firms"
##  [464] " that bypassed inspection, paid all cash and turne"
##  [465] "d all those houses into rentals, stealing away her"
##  [466] " American dream. She was devastated. Stories like "
##  [467] "this are why last month I signed executive order t"
##  [468] "o ban large Wall Street investment firms are buyin"
##  [469] "g up in the thousands single family homes. And now"
##  [470] " I’m asking Congress to make that ban permanent, b"
##  [471] "ecause homes for people, really that’s what we wan"
##  [472] "t, we want homes for people, not for corporations."
##  [473] " Corporations are doing just fine. Rachel, thank y"
##  [474] "ou very much. Good luck with your home. You’ll get"
##  [475] " one soon. We’re also working to make it easier fo"
##  [476] "r Americans to save for retirement. And under this"
##  [477] " administration, we will always protect Social Sec"
##  [478] "urity and Medicare. They are not protecting it for"
##  [479] " our seniors. We will always protect Social Securi"
##  [480] "ty, Medicare, Medicaid. Since I took office, the t"
##  [481] "ypical 401(k) balance is up by at least $30,000. T"
##  [482] "hat’s a lot of money. We have millions and million"
##  [483] "s of people. Because the stock market has done so "
##  [484] "well, setting all those records. Your 401(k)s are "
##  [485] "way up. Yet half of all of working Americans still"
##  [486] " do not have access to a retirement plan with matc"
##  [487] "hing contributions from an employer. To remedy thi"
##  [488] "s gross disparity, I’m announcing that next year m"
##  [489] "y administration will give these oft-forgotten Ame"
##  [490] "rican workers, great people, the people that built"
##  [491] " our country, access to the same type of retiremen"
##  [492] "t plan offered to every federal worker. We will ma"
##  [493] "tch your contribution with up to $1,000 each year,"
##  [494] " as we ensure that all Americans can profit from a"
##  [495] " rising stock market. Let’s also ensure that membe"
##  [496] "rs of Congress cannot corruptly profit from using "
##  [497] "insider information. They stood up for that. I can"
##  [498] "’t believe it. I can’t believe it. Did Nancy Pelos"
##  [499] "i stand up, if she’s here? Doubt it. Pass the Stop"
##  [500] " Insider Trading Act without delay. I wasn’t sure "
##  [501] "if anybody even on this side was going to applaud "
##  [502] "for that. I’m very impressed. Thank you. I’m very "
##  [503] "impressed. But when it comes to the corruption tha"
##  [504] "t is plundering, really, it’s plundering America, "
##  [505] "there’s been no more stunning example than Minneso"
##  [506] "ta, where members of the Somali community have pil"
##  [507] "laged an estimated $19 billion from the American t"
##  [508] "axpayer. We have all the information. And, in actu"
##  [509] "ality, the number is much higher than that. And Ca"
##  [510] "lifornia, Massachusetts, Maine and many other stat"
##  [511] "es are even worse. This is the kind of corruption "
##  [512] "that shreds the fabric of a nation, and we are wor"
##  [513] "king on it like you wouldn’t believe. So tonight, "
##  [514] "although started four months ago, I am officially "
##  [515] "announcing the war on fraud to be led by our great"
##  [516] " Vice President JD Vance. He’ll get it done. And w"
##  [517] "e’re able to find enough of that fraud, we will ac"
##  [518] "tually have a balanced budget overnight. It’ll go "
##  [519] "very quickly. That’s the kind of money you’re talk"
##  [520] "ing about. We’ll balance our budget. The Somali pi"
##  [521] "rates who ransacked Minnesota remind us that there"
##  [522] " are large parts of the world where bribery, corru"
##  [523] "ption, and lawlessness are the norm, not the excep"
##  [524] "tion. Importing these cultures through unrestricte"
##  [525] "d immigration and open borders brings us problems "
##  [526] "right here to the USA. And it is the American peop"
##  [527] "le who pay the price in higher medical bills, car "
##  [528] "insurance rates, rent, taxes, and perhaps most imp"
##  [529] "ortantly, crime. We will take care of this problem"
##  [530] ". We’re going to take care of this problem. We are"
##  [531] " not playing games. Dalilah. Dalilah Coleman was o"
##  [532] "nly five years old in June 2024, when a 18-wheel t"
##  [533] "ractor-trailer plowed into her stopped car, travel"
##  [534] "ing at 60 miles an hour or more. The driver was an"
##  [535] " illegal alien, let in by Joe Biden and given a co"
##  [536] "mmercial driver’s license by open borders, politic"
##  [537] "ians and California. Doctors said Dalilah would ne"
##  [538] "ver be able to walk or talk, have a good life. She"
##  [539] " wouldn’t even be able to eat again. But against a"
##  [540] "ll odds, she is now in the first grade, learning t"
##  [541] "o walk. And she’s here this evening with her dad, "
##  [542] "Marcus, a fantastic man. Dalilah, please. You are "
##  [543] "a great inspiration. Please stand up. Thank you, D"
##  [544] "alilah. Many, if not most, illegal aliens do not s"
##  [545] "peak English and cannot read even the most basic r"
##  [546] "oad signs as to direction, speed, danger or locati"
##  [547] "on. That’s why tonight I’m calling on Congress to "
##  [548] "pass what we will call the Dalilah law - barring a"
##  [549] "ny state from granting commercial driver’s license"
##  [550] "s to illegal aliens. And yesterday, as you probabl"
##  [551] "y saw at the White House, I hosted a ceremony with"
##  [552] " Americans who lost their treasured loved ones to "
##  [553] "the scourge of illegal immigration. People came in"
##  [554] "to our country. How we allowed this to happen with"
##  [555] " our open borders. These are the angel moms and fa"
##  [556] "milies that for decades our government betrayed an"
##  [557] "d our media totally ignored. Totally. It was terri"
##  [558] "ble. Hard to believe, actually. In 2023, a 16-year"
##  [559] "-old high school cheerleader named Lizbeth Medina "
##  [560] "was supposed to perform in her town’s Christmas pa"
##  [561] "rade, but she never arrived. Her mother, Jacquelin"
##  [562] "e, went home to look for her, and she found her ly"
##  [563] "ing dead in a bathtub, bleeding profusely after be"
##  [564] "ing stabbed 25 times. Lizbeth’s killer was a previ"
##  [565] "ously arrested illegal alien who had broken in and"
##  [566] " brutally, just brutally extinguished the brightes"
##  [567] "t light in her family’s life. Violently and viciou"
##  [568] "sly. Her heartbroken mother is in the gallery to r"
##  [569] "emind everyone in this chamber exactly why we are "
##  [570] "deporting illegal alien criminals. Our country had"
##  [571] " record numbers, and we’re getting them the hell o"
##  [572] "ut of here fast. We don’t want ‘em. Thank you very"
##  [573] " much, Jacqueline. Thank you. We can never forget "
##  [574] "that many in this room not only allowed the border"
##  [575] " invasion to happen before I got involved, but ind"
##  [576] "eed, they would do it all over again if they ever "
##  [577] "had the chance. If they ever got elected, they wou"
##  [578] "ld open up those borders to some of the worst crim"
##  [579] "inals anywhere in the world. The only thing standi"
##  [580] "ng between Americans and a wide-open border right "
##  [581] "now is President Donald J. Trump and our great Rep"
##  [582] "ublican patriots in Congress. Thank you. As we spe"
##  [583] "ak, Democrats in this chamber have cut off all fun"
##  [584] "ding for the Department of Homeland Security. It’s"
##  [585] " all cut off, all cut off. They have instituted an"
##  [586] "other Democrat shutdown, the first one costing us "
##  [587] "two points on GDP. Two points we lost on GDP, whic"
##  [588] "h probably made them quite happy actually. Now the"
##  [589] "y have closed the agency responsible for protectin"
##  [590] "g Americans from terrorists and murderers. Tonight"
##  [591] ", I’m demanding the full and immediate restoration"
##  [592] " of all funding for the border security, homeland "
##  [593] "security of the United States, and also for helpin"
##  [594] "g people clean up their snow. We have no money bec"
##  [595] "ause of the Democrats, and it would be nice. We’d "
##  [596] "love to give you a hand at cleaning it up, but you"
##  [597] " gave no money. Nobody’s getting paid. It’s a sham"
##  [598] "e. So you have to think about it. We have, in case"
##  [599] " you didn’t know, a pretty large snowstorm out the"
##  [600] "re. One of the great things about the State of the"
##  [601] " Union is how it gives Americans the chance to see"
##  [602] " clearly what their representatives really believe"
##  [603] ". So tonight, I’m inviting every legislator to joi"
##  [604] "n with my administration in reaffirming a fundamen"
##  [605] "tal principle. If you agree with this statement, t"
##  [606] "hen stand up and show your support: The first duty"
##  [607] " of the American government is to protect American"
##  [608] " citizens, not illegal aliens. Isn’t that a shame?"
##  [609] " You should be ashamed of yourself not standing up"
##  [610] ". You should be ashamed of yourself. That is why I"
##  [611] "’m also asking you to end deadly sanctuary cities "
##  [612] "that protect the criminals and enact serious penal"
##  [613] "ties for public officials who block the removal of"
##  [614] " criminal aliens. In many cases, drug lords, murde"
##  [615] "rers all over our country. They’re blocking the re"
##  [616] "moval of these people out of our country. And you "
##  [617] "should be ashamed of yourself. And perhaps most im"
##  [618] "portantly, I’m asking you to approve the SAVE Amer"
##  [619] "ica Act, to stop illegal aliens and others who are"
##  [620] " unpermitted persons from voting in our sacred Ame"
##  [621] "rican elections, the cheating is rampant in our el"
##  [622] "ections. It’s rampant. It’s very simple. All voter"
##  [623] "s must show voter ID. All voters must show proof o"
##  [624] "f citizenship in order to vote. And no more crooke"
##  [625] "d mail-in ballots except for illness, disability, "
##  [626] "military or travel. None. And this should be an ea"
##  [627] "sy one and, by the way, is polling at 89%, includi"
##  [628] "ng Democrats, 89%. And even the new communist mayo"
##  [629] "r of New York City. I think he’s a nice guy, actua"
##  [630] "lly speak to him a lot. Bad policy, but nice guy. "
##  [631] "Just said they want people to shovel snow. They go"
##  [632] "t hit hard. Wants them to shovel snow. But if you "
##  [633] "apply for that job, you need to show two original "
##  [634] "forms of ID and a Social Security card. Yet they d"
##  [635] "on’t want identification for the greatest privileg"
##  [636] "e of them all: voting in America. No, it’s no good"
##  [637] ", no good. Both Republicans and Democrats overwhel"
##  [638] "mingly agree on the policy that we just enunciated"
##  [639] ", and Congress should unite and enact this common "
##  [640] "sense, country saving legislation right now. And i"
##  [641] "t should be before anything else happens. And the "
##  [642] "reason they don’t want to do it, why would anybody"
##  [643] " not want voter ID? One reason, because they want "
##  [644] "to cheat. There’s only one reason. They make up al"
##  [645] "l excuses. They say it’s racist. They come up with"
##  [646] " things. You almost say what imagination they have"
##  [647] ". They want to cheat, they have cheated, and their"
##  [648] " policy is so bad that the only way they can get e"
##  [649] "lected is to cheat. And we’re going to stop it. We"
##  [650] " have to stop it, John. And here is one more oppor"
##  [651] "tunity to show common sense in government. In the "
##  [652] "gallery tonight are Sage Blair and her mother, Mic"
##  [653] "hele. In 2021, Sage was 14 when school officials i"
##  [654] "n Virginia sought to socially transition her to a "
##  [655] "new gender, treating her as a boy and hiding it fr"
##  [656] "om her parents. Hard to believe, isn’t it? Before "
##  [657] "long, a confused Sage ran away from home. After sh"
##  [658] "e was found in a horrific situation in Maryland, a"
##  [659] " left-wing judge refused to return Sage to her par"
##  [660] "ents because they did not immediately state that t"
##  [661] "heir daughter was their son. Sage was thrown into "
##  [662] "an all-boys state home and suffered terribly for a"
##  [663] " long time. But today, all of that is behind them."
##  [664] " Because Sage is a proud and wonderful young woman"
##  [665] " with a full ride scholarship to Liberty Universit"
##  [666] "y. Sage and Rachelle, please stand up. And thank y"
##  [667] "ou for your great bravery. And who can believe tha"
##  [668] "t we’re even speaking about things like this? 15 y"
##  [669] "ears ago, if somebody was up here and said that, t"
##  [670] "hey’d say, “What’s wrong with him?” But now we hav"
##  [671] "e to say it because it’s going on all over numerou"
##  [672] "s states. They’re not even telling the parents. Bu"
##  [673] "t surely we can all agree no state can be allowed "
##  [674] "to rip children from their parents’ arms and trans"
##  [675] "ition them to a new gender against the parents’ wi"
##  [676] "ll. Who would believe that we’re even talking abou"
##  [677] "t it? We must ban it, and we must ban it immediate"
##  [678] "ly. Nobody stands up, these people are crazy. I’m "
##  [679] "telling them they’re crazy. Amazing. Boy, oh boy. "
##  [680] "We’re lucky we have a country with people like thi"
##  [681] "s -- Democrats are destroying our country. But we’"
##  [682] "ve stopped it just in the nick of time, didn’t we?"
##  [683] " No one cares more about protecting America’s yout"
##  [684] "h than our wonderful First Lady. Now a movie star,"
##  [685] " a movie star. Who would have believed that? Over "
##  [686] "the past year, she has had an incredible impact ch"
##  [687] "ampioning AI legislation, advancing a landmark exe"
##  [688] "cutive order on foster care, and helping secure $3"
##  [689] "0 million to launch the Melania Trump Foster Youth"
##  [690] " to Independence Initiative. It’s a tremendous, re"
##  [691] "ally a tremendous thing that happened and had a lo"
##  [692] "t of bipartisan support. She gets much better bipa"
##  [693] "rtisan support than I do. I get none, she gets a l"
##  [694] "ot. Someday, you’re gonna have to tell me how you "
##  [695] "did that. And students and educators in every stat"
##  [696] "e have joined the First Lady’s efforts in the pres"
##  [697] "idential AI challenge, keeping America’s next gene"
##  [698] "ration positioned to succeed and strongly succeed "
##  [699] "in the future. Tonight, we welcome two young peopl"
##  [700] "e whose lives reflect the First Lady’s impact: Sie"
##  [701] "rra Burns and Everest Nevraumont. Thank you both. "
##  [702] "And Melania, thank you. I know how hard you worked"
##  [703] " on it. Thank you very. I’m very proud to say that"
##  [704] " during my time in office, both the first four yea"
##  [705] "rs and in particular this last year, there has bee"
##  [706] "n a tremendous renewal in religion, faith, Christi"
##  [707] "anity and belief in God. This is especially true a"
##  [708] "mong young people, and a big part of that had to d"
##  [709] "o with my great friend Charlie Kirk. Great guy. Gr"
##  [710] "eat guy. So last year, Charlie was violently murde"
##  [711] "red by an assassin. And martyred, really, martyred"
##  [712] " for his beliefs. His wonderful wife, Erika is wit"
##  [713] "h us tonight. Erika, please stand. Thank you. Erik"
##  [714] "a, thank you a lot. In Charlie’s memory, we must a"
##  [715] "ll come together to reaffirm that America is one n"
##  [716] "ation under God. And we must totally reject politi"
##  [717] "cal violence of any kind. We love religion, and we"
##  [718] " love bringing it back. And it’s coming back at le"
##  [719] "vels that nobody actually thought possible. It’s r"
##  [720] "eally a beautiful thing to see. Above all, unleash"
##  [721] "ing America’s promise requires keeping our communi"
##  [722] "ties safe. We have made incredible strides, yet da"
##  [723] "ngerous repeat offenders continue to be released b"
##  [724] "y pro-crime Democrat politicians again and again. "
##  [725] "We are honored to be joined tonight by a woman who"
##  [726] "’s been through hell, Anna Zarutska. In 2022, she "
##  [727] "and her beautiful daughter — so beautiful, what a "
##  [728] "beautiful young woman — Iryna fled war-torn, war-t"
##  [729] "orn Ukraine to live with relatives near Charlotte,"
##  [730] " North Carolina. And by the way, what’s going on w"
##  [731] "ith Charlotte? Last summer, 23-year-old Iryna was "
##  [732] "riding home on the train when a deranged monster w"
##  [733] "ho had been arrested over a dozen times and was re"
##  [734] "leased through no cash bail, stood up and viciousl"
##  [735] "y slashed a knife through her neck and body. No on"
##  [736] "e will ever forget. There were people on that trai"
##  [737] "n. No one will ever forget the expression of terro"
##  [738] "r on Iryna’s face as she looked up at her attacker"
##  [739] " in the last seconds of her life. She died instant"
##  [740] "ly. She had escaped a brutal war, only to be slain"
##  [741] " by a hardened criminal set free to kill in Americ"
##  [742] "a. Came in through open borders. Mrs. Zarutska, to"
##  [743] "night, I promise you, we will ensure justice for y"
##  [744] "our magnificent daughter, Iryna. How do you not st"
##  [745] "and, how do you not stand? I’m asking this Congres"
##  [746] "s to pass tough legislation to ensure that violent"
##  [747] " and dangerous repeat offenders are put behind bar"
##  [748] "s, and, importantly, that they stay there. Startin"
##  [749] "g last summer, I deployed our National Guard and f"
##  [750] "ederal law enforcement to restore law and order to"
##  [751] " our most dangerous cities, including Memphis, Ten"
##  [752] "nessee -- big success. New Orleans, Louisiana. A b"
##  [753] "ig success in our nation’s capital itself, Washing"
##  [754] "ton, D.C., where we have almost no crime anymore i"
##  [755] "n Washington, D.C. How did that happen? In fact, c"
##  [756] "rime in Washington is now at the lowest level ever"
##  [757] " recorded, and murders in D.C. this January were d"
##  [758] "own close to 100% from a year ago. They don’t like"
##  [759] " to hear that. One of the brave service members wh"
##  [760] "o helped achieve this stunning turnaround was 20-y"
##  [761] "ear-old West Virginia Army National Guard Speciali"
##  [762] "st Sarah Beckstrom. After a four-month deployment,"
##  [763] " she voluntarily extended her service. Her rank wa"
##  [764] "s going to be lifted and she was doing so well and"
##  [765] " they were so proud of her. But the very next day,"
##  [766] " she was on patrol in the White House when she was"
##  [767] " ambushed and shot in the head by a terrorist mons"
##  [768] "ter from Afghanistan. Shouldn’t have been in our c"
##  [769] "ountry. And all because she wore the uniform of ou"
##  [770] "r nation. She was shot. He traveled here because h"
##  [771] "e didn’t like people wearing our uniform. He was s"
##  [772] "ick, and deranged. Shouldn’t have been in our coun"
##  [773] "try. Sarah Beckstrom died in order to defend our c"
##  [774] "apital, and we are honored to be joined by her won"
##  [775] "derful parents, Gary and Evalea. Your daughter was"
##  [776] " a true American patriot and she will be greatly m"
##  [777] "issed. She was a great person. I saw reports on he"
##  [778] "r, they’ve never seen anything like it. So sorry. "
##  [779] "Thank you very much. A great young lady. I saw rep"
##  [780] "orts they were like, perfect, she’s perfect. Servi"
##  [781] "ng alongside Sarah that day was Staff Sgt. Andrew "
##  [782] "Wolfe. The terrorist shot Andrew in the head, and "
##  [783] "no one thought he could possibly make it. The two "
##  [784] "of them, Sarah and Andrew, shot violently in the h"
##  [785] "ead. Neither was expected to make it. They weren’t"
##  [786] " even given any chance. Except his wonderful mothe"
##  [787] "r, named Melanie, who I spoke to the same night, a"
##  [788] "nd she was so positive. The doctors thought that A"
##  [789] "ndrew was gone, but his mother said, “No, no, Mr. "
##  [790] "President, Andrew will be be fine. He’s going to m"
##  [791] "ake it.” I’ve never seen anything like it. I mean,"
##  [792] " he was given almost no chance. She said, “I have "
##  [793] "no doubt, Sir, he’s going to be okay.” This was th"
##  [794] "e conversation that I had with her that night, wit"
##  [795] "h her son laying in bed, blood all over. Everybody"
##  [796] " is praying. She said, “Sir, he will be okay.” The"
##  [797] " doctors didn’t understand what she was saying and"
##  [798] " after looking at the results of the damage done, "
##  [799] "neither did I. She was so strong and conclusive th"
##  [800] "at even Andrew’s great father felt she didn’t real"
##  [801] "ly understand the gravity of the situation, but sh"
##  [802] "e turned out to be right. Right, Melanie? She turn"
##  [803] "ed out to be right. Amazing actually. I said “wher"
##  [804] "e does this woman come from, she’s the most positi"
##  [805] "ve person I’ve ever met?” With God’s help, Andrew "
##  [806] "has battled back from the edge of death. And we’re"
##  [807] " talking about the edge of death, on his way to a "
##  [808] "miraculous recovery. He’s got a little work to do,"
##  [809] " but he’s doing great. Nice to see you -- he’s a g"
##  [810] "ood-looking guy. Nice to see you. Thanks, Andrew. "
##  [811] "Thank you very much. So Andrew now you’re up, I’m "
##  [812] "going to ask a highly respected Gen. James Seward "
##  [813] "to present Staff Sergeant Andrew Wolfe and the gre"
##  [814] "at family of Sarah Beckstrom with the award create"
##  [815] "d by our late, great president, George Washington "
##  [816] "himself. It’s called the Purple Heart. We love you"
##  [817] " all. Love you. We’re proudly restoring safety for"
##  [818] " Americans at home, and we are also restoring secu"
##  [819] "rity for Americans abroad. Our country has never b"
##  [820] "een stronger. My first 10 months, I ended eight wa"
##  [821] "rs, including Cambodia. Isn’t it funny? You sick p"
##  [822] "eople. Cambodia and Thailand. Pakistan and India w"
##  [823] "ould have been a nuclear war. 35 million people sa"
##  [824] "id the Prime Minister of Pakistan would have died "
##  [825] "if it were not for my involvement. Kosovo and Serb"
##  [826] "ia, Israel and Iran, Egypt and Ethiopia. Armenia a"
##  [827] "nd Azerbaijan, the Congo and Rwanda. And of course"
##  [828] ", the war in Gaza, which proceeds at a very low le"
##  [829] "vel. It’s just about there. And I want to thank St"
##  [830] "eve Witkoff and Jared Kushner for your help. Thank"
##  [831] " you Steve. Thank you Jared. And I also want to th"
##  [832] "ank the man they report to Secretary of State. Mar"
##  [833] "co Rubio. Thank you. Thank. People like you. You k"
##  [834] "now, Marco got 100% of the votes when he was in co"
##  [835] "nfirmation -- I think our next one was about 54%. "
##  [836] "And some of the Democrats are now saying, I can’t "
##  [837] "believe we approved that guy. And he said, it’s an"
##  [838] " honor that they feel that way. Right, Marco? You "
##  [839] "have done a great job, great Secretary of state. I"
##  [840] " think he’ll go down as the best ever. Under the c"
##  [841] "ease fire, I negotiated every single hostage, both"
##  [842] " living and dead, has been returned home. Can you "
##  [843] "believe that? Nobody thought it was possible. Nobo"
##  [844] "dy thought that was possible. Both living and dead"
##  [845] ". And those parents who had a dead son, their boy,"
##  [846] " they’d always tell me their boy. They wanted him "
##  [847] "as much as though he were living. That was an amaz"
##  [848] "ing period of time. And they came back. And when w"
##  [849] "e got all of the living hostages back and many, ma"
##  [850] "ny before them. But I always said those last 20 ar"
##  [851] "e going to be very tough. But we got many, many mo"
##  [852] "re hundreds. But I said those last 20 are going to"
##  [853] " be tough. We got them back, but we only got back "
##  [854] "14 or 15 of the dead of the 28 And believe it or n"
##  [855] "ot, Hamas worked along with Israel and they dug an"
##  [856] "d they dug and they dug. It’s a tough -- it’s a to"
##  [857] "ugh thing to do, going through bodies all over, pa"
##  [858] "ssing up 100 bodies sometimes for each one that th"
##  [859] "ey found. Tough job and they finally got it back t"
##  [860] "o 27 and then Steve and Jared they got it back to "
##  [861] "28. They found all 28 nobody thought that was poss"
##  [862] "ible but we did it and I remember the family of th"
##  [863] "e 28th they were so grieved but they were so happy"
##  [864] " as happy as it’s possible to be they had their bo"
##  [865] "y back. The mother said ‘sir we have our boy back."
##  [866] "’ What a period of time that was, but we got them "
##  [867] "all back, so thank you both very much. And we’re w"
##  [868] "orking very hard to end the ninth war, the killing"
##  [869] " and slaughter between Russia and Ukraine, where 2"
##  [870] "5,000 soldiers are dying each and every month — th"
##  [871] "ink of that, 25,000 soldiers are dying a month. A "
##  [872] "war which would have never happened if I were pres"
##  [873] "ident. Would have never happened. As president, I "
##  [874] "will make peace wherever I can, but I will never h"
##  [875] "esitate to confront threats to America wherever we"
##  [876] " must. That’s why in a breakthrough operation last"
##  [877] " June, the United States military obliterated Iran"
##  [878] "’s nuclear weapons program with an attack on Irani"
##  [879] "an soil known as Operation Midnight Hammer. For de"
##  [880] "cades, it has been the policy of the United States"
##  [881] " never to allow Iran to obtain a nuclear weapon. M"
##  [882] "any decades. Since they seized control of that pro"
##  [883] "ud nation 47 years ago, the regime and its murdero"
##  [884] "us proxies have spread nothing but terrorism and d"
##  [885] "eath and hate. They’ve killed and maimed thousands"
##  [886] " of American service members and hundreds of thous"
##  [887] "ands and even millions of people. With what’s call"
##  [888] "ed roadside bombs. They were the kings of the road"
##  [889] "side bomb. And we took out Soleimani. I did that d"
##  [890] "uring my first term. Had a huge impact. He was the"
##  [891] " father of the roadside bomb. And just over the la"
##  [892] "st couple of months with the protests, they’ve kil"
##  [893] "led at least, it looks like, 32,000 protesters — 3"
##  [894] "2,000 protesters in their own country. They shot t"
##  [895] "hem and hung them. We stopped them from hanging a "
##  [896] "lot of them with the threat of serious violence. B"
##  [897] "ut this is some terrible people. They’ve already d"
##  [898] "eveloped missiles that can threaten Europe and our"
##  [899] " bases overseas, and they’re working to build miss"
##  [900] "iles that will soon reach the United States of Ame"
##  [901] "rica. After Midnight Hammer, they were warned to m"
##  [902] "ake no future attempts to rebuild their weapons pr"
##  [903] "ogram, and in particular nuclear weapons, yet they"
##  [904] " continue. They’re starting it all over. We wiped "
##  [905] "it out and they want to start it all over again an"
##  [906] "d are at this moment again pursuing their sinister"
##  [907] " ambitions. We are in negotiations with them. They"
##  [908] " want to make a deal, but we haven’t heard those s"
##  [909] "ecret words, “We will never have a nuclear weapon."
##  [910] "” My preference, my preference is to solve this pr"
##  [911] "oblem through diplomacy. But one thing is certain,"
##  [912] " I will never allow the world’s No. 1 sponsor of t"
##  [913] "error, which they are by far, to have a nuclear we"
##  [914] "apon. Can’t let that happen. And no nation should "
##  [915] "ever doubt America’s resolve. We have the most pow"
##  [916] "erful military on earth. I rebuilt the military in"
##  [917] " my first term. We’re going to continue to do so a"
##  [918] "lso, which is to prove a trillion-dollar budget. W"
##  [919] "e have no choice. We have to be strong, because ho"
##  [920] "pefully we will seldom have to use this great powe"
##  [921] "r that we built together. It’s really called peace"
##  [922] " through strength, that has been very, very effect"
##  [923] "ive. So thanks to Republicans in Congress, we’re i"
##  [924] "nvesting that record number of dollars — we have n"
##  [925] "o choice — in the United States Armed Forces, also"
##  [926] " creating a lot of jobs, but we’re not even doing "
##  [927] "it for that reason. Because as I said, we have mor"
##  [928] "e jobs, more people working today than ever before"
##  [929] " in the history of our country. And NATO countries"
##  [930] ", our friends and allies — they are, they’re our f"
##  [931] "riends and they’re our allies — have just agreed, "
##  [932] "at my very strong request, to pay 5% of GDP for mi"
##  [933] "litary defense rather than the 2%, which they were"
##  [934] "n’t paying, we were paying for almost all of that."
##  [935] " Now they’re paying 5, as opposed to not paying 2."
##  [936] " And getting that 5% was something which everyone "
##  [937] "said would never be done, could not happen. We got"
##  [938] " it really easily with one meeting. And a big diff"
##  [939] "erence between 2% that’s not paid, we were paying "
##  [940] "the freight of many of them, very few were paid up"
##  [941] ", now 5% then they’re paid. And everything we send"
##  [942] " over to Ukraine is sent through NATO and they pay"
##  [943] " us in full. They pay us totally in full, every br"
##  [944] "anch of our Armed Forces is setting records for re"
##  [945] "cruitment. This is so exciting. And every service "
##  [946] "member. And every service member recently received"
##  [947] " a warrior dividend of $1,776. You know, they clea"
##  [948] "red up my desk, we got the money from tariffs and "
##  [949] "other things, a lot of money we have with, much mo"
##  [950] "re money than people understand, have to rebuild t"
##  [951] "hat program a little bit but it won’t take long. B"
##  [952] "ut we got all the money and it was $1,775 and they"
##  [953] " wanted my approval. And I said, what’s the number"
##  [954] "? $1,775. I said, wait a minute, for one more doll"
##  [955] "ar, we can have $1,776. I said, we’re going to fig"
##  [956] "ure that out. I never asked anybody if we could af"
##  [957] "ford it. One more dollar. I said $1,776. And I sai"
##  [958] "d that’s good. And I’ll tell you what, our militar"
##  [959] "y, that was four months ago, I never see a person "
##  [960] "in the military that doesn’t thank me for it. So w"
##  [961] "e’re honored to do it. They deserve it. And we cal"
##  [962] "l it “1776.” It was great. And we love our militar"
##  [963] "y. We love our law enforcement. We love a fireman."
##  [964] " The firemen don’t get mentioned enough. We love f"
##  [965] "iremen. We’re also restoring American security and"
##  [966] " dominance in the Western Hemisphere, acting to se"
##  [967] "cure our national interests and defend our country"
##  [968] " from violence, drugs, terrorism, and foreign inte"
##  [969] "rference for years. Large swaths of territory in o"
##  [970] "ur region, including large parts of Mexico, really"
##  [971] " large parts of Mexico, have been controlled by mu"
##  [972] "rderous drug cartels. That’s why I designated thes"
##  [973] "e cartels as foreign terrorist organizations and I"
##  [974] " declared illicit fentanyl as a weapon of mass des"
##  [975] "truction. And with our new military campaign, we h"
##  [976] "ave stopped record amounts of drugs coming into ou"
##  [977] "r country and virtually stopped it completely comi"
##  [978] "ng in by water or sea, you probably noticed that. "
##  [979] "You’ve very seriously damaged their fishing indust"
##  [980] "ry, also. Nobody wants to go fishing anymore. We’v"
##  [981] "e also taken down one of the most sinister cartel "
##  [982] "kingpins of all. You saw that yesterday. In Januar"
##  [983] "y, in January, elite American warriors carried out"
##  [984] " one of the most complex, spectacular feats of mil"
##  [985] "itary competence and power in world history. No on"
##  [986] "e’s seen anything like it. Foreign leaders, I won’"
##  [987] "t tell you who, called me and they said, very impr"
##  [988] "essive, very impressive. They couldn’t believe, th"
##  [989] "ey all watched, they saw what happened. This is a "
##  [990] "different fighting force than we had years ago whe"
##  [991] "n we fought to tie. You know, it’s a great fightin"
##  [992] "g force. I’m so proud of it. Look at Space Force. "
##  [993] "Space Force is my baby. As we did that, my baby is"
##  [994] " becoming so important. And America’s Armed Forces"
##  [995] " overwhelmed all defenses and utterly defeated a a"
##  [996] "n enemy — good fighters — to end the reign of outl"
##  [997] "awed dictator Nicolas Maduro and bring him to face"
##  [998] " American justice. And this was an absolutely a co"
##  [999] "lossal victory for the security of the United Stat"
## [1000] "es. A bright new beginning for the people of Venez"
## [1001] "uela. We’re working closely with the new president"
## [1002] " of Venezuela, Delcy Rodríguez, to unleash extraor"
## [1003] "dinary economic gains for both of our countries an"
## [1004] "d to bring new hope to those who have suffered so "
## [1005] "terribly. They really did suffer. With us tonight "
## [1006] "is Alejandro Gonzalez. She grew up in a tight-knit"
## [1007] " Venezuelan family and was especially close to her"
## [1008] " beloved Uncle Enrique. But after Enrique ran for "
## [1009] "office and opposed Maduro, he was kidnapped by Mad"
## [1010] "uro’s security forces and thrown into the regime’s"
## [1011] " really infamous prison in Caracas. Alejandro fear"
## [1012] "ed she would never see her uncle again. She feared"
## [1013] " for her own life, also. But since the raid, we ha"
## [1014] "ve worked with the new leadership, and they have o"
## [1015] "rdered the closure of that vile prison and release"
## [1016] "d hundreds of political prisoners already with mor"
## [1017] "e to come. Alejandro, I’m pleased to inform you th"
## [1018] "at not only has your uncle been released, but he i"
## [1019] "s here tonight. We brought him over to celebrate h"
## [1020] "is freedom with you in person. Enrique, please com"
## [1021] "e down. Thank you, Enrique. Have a good time. Nice"
## [1022] " to have you back, Enrique. There were many heroes"
## [1023] " on that January raid to capture Maduro. Really gr"
## [1024] "eat heroes. It was very dangerous. They knew we we"
## [1025] "re coming. They were all set. But the deeds of one"
## [1026] " warrior that night will live forever in the etern"
## [1027] "al chronicles of military valor. Chief Warrant Off"
## [1028] "icer 5 Eric Slover planned the mission and was the"
## [1029] " flight lead in the cockpit of the first helicopte"
## [1030] "r. A big, beautiful, powerful helicopter. Was a ma"
## [1031] "ssive Chinook carrying, as you can imagine, many, "
## [1032] "many American warfighters wearing the dog tags his"
## [1033] " wife Amy had blessed with holy water before he le"
## [1034] "ft. She knew it was going to be a rough one. Eric "
## [1035] "steered the Chinook under the cover of night and d"
## [1036] "escended swiftly upon Maduro’s heavily protected m"
## [1037] "ilitary fortress. This was a major military instal"
## [1038] "lation, protected by thousands of soldiers and gua"
## [1039] "rded by Russian and Chinese military technology. H"
## [1040] "ow did that work out? Not too good. While preparin"
## [1041] "g to land, enemy machine guns fired from every ang"
## [1042] "le. And Eric was hit, very badly, in the leg and h"
## [1043] "ip, one bullet after another. He absorbed four ago"
## [1044] "nizing shots, shredding his leg into numerous piec"
## [1045] "es. And yet, despite the fact that the use of his "
## [1046] "legs was vital to successful helicopter flight, Mi"
## [1047] "ke said the most important part of flying a helico"
## [1048] "pter to deliver the many commanders who would capt"
## [1049] "ure and detain Maduro was the only thing Eric was "
## [1050] "thinking about. And even as he was gushing blood, "
## [1051] "which was flowing back down the aisle - helicopter"
## [1052] " lands at a steep angle. The machine guns stood ri"
## [1053] "ght in front of him. They were right in front of h"
## [1054] "im. Two machine-gunners who escaped the wrath of t"
## [1055] "he previous planes. Eric maneuvered his helicopter"
## [1056] " with all of those lives and souls to face the ene"
## [1057] "my, and let his gunners eliminate the threat, turn"
## [1058] " the helicopter around so the gunners could take c"
## [1059] "are of business, saving the lives of his fellow wa"
## [1060] "rriors from what could have been a catastrophic cr"
## [1061] "ash deep in enemy territory. Only after safely lan"
## [1062] "ding the helicopter with all the warriors aboard i"
## [1063] "n the exact right spot, which was vital to the mis"
## [1064] "sion, probably would have happened. Maybe cancel t"
## [1065] "he mission if that didn’t happen. Eric told his co"
## [1066] "pilot, also wounded, but not as gravely, to take o"
## [1067] "ver. I’m about ready to pass out. The success of t"
## [1068] "he entire mission, and the lives of his fellow war"
## [1069] "riors hinge on Eric’s ability to take searing pain"
## [1070] ". It was unbelievable what’s happened to his legs."
## [1071] " Of the bullets and keep on flying and landing. Pe"
## [1072] "ople knew what was happening. Everybody in the bac"
## [1073] "k of the helicopter knew because they saw the bloo"
## [1074] "d pouring down the aisle. Chief Warrant Officer Sl"
## [1075] "over is still recovering from his serious wounds, "
## [1076] "but I’m thrilled to say that he is here tonight wi"
## [1077] "th his wife, Amy. Eric and Amy, come on in. So we "
## [1078] "have a surprise, Eric and Amy. In recognition of E"
## [1079] "ric’s actions above and beyond the call of duty, I"
## [1080] " would now like to ask General Jonathan Braga to p"
## [1081] "resent Chief Warrant Officer Slover with our natio"
## [1082] "n’s highest military award, the Congressional Meda"
## [1083] "l of Honor. Well, thank you very much, Eric and Am"
## [1084] "y. Great to get to know you. I met with them and w"
## [1085] "ith a lot of their fellow warriors at Fort Bragg r"
## [1086] "ecently. You notice the name Fort Bragg. We have i"
## [1087] "t back. We brought it back. We won the First World"
## [1088] " War with it, the Second World War with it, and th"
## [1089] "en they decided to change the name. But we changed"
## [1090] " it back. Everybody wanted it changed back too. An"
## [1091] "d 10 of Eric’s fellow warriors from that incredibl"
## [1092] "e night of victory will also be receiving medals a"
## [1093] "t a private ceremony that will soon be held at the"
## [1094] " White House. And it will be the. Thank you, Eric."
## [1095] " That’s a big one. Tonight we’ve celebrated many t"
## [1096] "ruly extraordinary American patriots. But there is"
## [1097] " one last living legend to honor before we go. He "
## [1098] "is one more heroic American aviator, Navy fighter "
## [1099] "pilot Royce Williams served in World War II, Korea"
## [1100] ", Vietnam, flying more than 220 missions in the sk"
## [1101] "ies over Korea. In 1952, Royce was in the dogfight"
## [1102] " of a lifetime. Legendary dogfight. Flying through"
## [1103] " blizzard conditions, his squadron was ambushed by"
## [1104] " seven Soviet fighter planes. It was his first aer"
## [1105] "ial combat of the war, and despite being massively"
## [1106] " outnumbered and outgunned, Royce led the takedown"
## [1107] " of four enemy jets and almost destroyed the other"
## [1108] "s, vanquishing his adversaries while taking 263 bu"
## [1109] "llets to his own plane and being seriously hurt. H"
## [1110] "is story was secret for over 50 years. He didn’t e"
## [1111] "ven want to tell his wife, but the legend grew and"
## [1112] " grew. But tonight, at 100 years old, his brave Na"
## [1113] "vy captain is finally getting the recognition he d"
## [1114] "eserves. He was a legend long before this evening."
## [1115] " Royce, please stand up and I will ask the first l"
## [1116] "ady of the United States to present Captain Royce "
## [1117] "Williams with his Congressional Medal of Honor. Th"
## [1118] "ank you Eric. I’ve always wanted the Congressional"
## [1119] " Medal of Honor, but I was informed I’m not allowe"
## [1120] "d to give it to myself, and I wouldn’t know why I’"
## [1121] "d be taking it. But if they ever opened up that la"
## [1122] "w I will be there with you someday. But you know, "
## [1123] "that’s our highest honor, the Congressional Medal "
## [1124] "of Honor. And that’s a big thing. And it’s an hono"
## [1125] "r to be in the same room with you. Thank you both "
## [1126] "very much. Thank you. Thank you. Two-hundred fifty"
## [1127] " years is a long time in the life of a nation. But"
## [1128] " in another sense, it’s really a mere moment in th"
## [1129] "e eye of history. Two of the gentlemen we met in t"
## [1130] "he gallery this evening took their first breaths o"
## [1131] "ne century ago. One hundred years before that, on "
## [1132] "July 4th, 1826, the author of the Declaration of I"
## [1133] "ndependence, brilliant Thomas Jefferson, drew his "
## [1134] "last breath. Just a single long human life span se"
## [1135] "parates the giants who declared and won our indepe"
## [1136] "ndence from the heroes who stand among us tonight."
## [1137] " Everything our nation has done, everything we hav"
## [1138] "e achieved, has been the work of those few great l"
## [1139] "ifetimes. In those brief chapters, Americans built"
## [1140] " this nation from 13 humble colonies into the pinn"
## [1141] "acle of human civilization and human freedom. The "
## [1142] "strongest, wealthiest, most powerful, most success"
## [1143] "ful nation in all of history. Americans ventured o"
## [1144] "ut across the daunting and dangerous continent. We"
## [1145] " carved pass through an unforgiving wilderness, se"
## [1146] "ttled a boundless frontier, and tamed the beautifu"
## [1147] "l but very, very dangerous wild west. From empty m"
## [1148] "arshes and wide-open plains, we raised up the worl"
## [1149] "d’s greatest cities. Together we mastered the worl"
## [1150] "d’s mightiest industries, shattered history’s mons"
## [1151] "trous tyrannies. And we liberated millions from th"
## [1152] "e chains of fascism, communism, oppression and ter"
## [1153] "ror. Americans lifted humanity into the skies or t"
## [1154] "he wings of aluminum and steel. And then we launch"
## [1155] "ed mankind into the stars on rockets powered by sh"
## [1156] "eer American will and unyielding American pride. W"
## [1157] "e wired the globe with our ingenuity. We captivate"
## [1158] "d the planet with American culture and now we are "
## [1159] "pioneering the next great American breakthroughs t"
## [1160] "hat will change the entire world. All of this, and"
## [1161] " so much more, is the enduring legacy, unmatched g"
## [1162] "lory of the hard-working patriots who built and de"
## [1163] "fended this country and who still carry the hopes "
## [1164] "and freedoms on all of humanity’s backs. For years"
## [1165] ", they were forgotten, betrayed, and cast aside. B"
## [1166] "ut that great betrayal is over, and they will neve"
## [1167] "r be forgotten again. Because when the world needs"
## [1168] " courage, daring, vision and inspiration, it is st"
## [1169] "ill turning to America. And when God needs a natio"
## [1170] "n to work his miracles, he knows exactly who to as"
## [1171] "k. There is no challenge Americans cannot overcome"
## [1172] ", no frontier too vast for us to conquer, no dream"
## [1173] " too bold for us to chase, no horizon too distant "
## [1174] "for us to claim. For our destiny is written by the"
## [1175] " hand of Providence. And these first 250 years wer"
## [1176] "e just the beginning. From the rugged border towns"
## [1177] " of Texas to the heartland villages of Michigan, f"
## [1178] "rom the sun-kissed shores of Florida to the endles"
## [1179] "s hills of the Dakotas, and from the historic stre"
## [1180] "ets of Philadelphia to right here in our nation’s "
## [1181] "capital, Washington, D.C., the golden age of Ameri"
## [1182] "ca is upon us. The revolution that began in 1776 h"
## [1183] "as not ended. It still continues because the flame"
## [1184] " of liberty and independence still burns in the he"
## [1185] "art of every American patriot, and our future will"
## [1186] " be bigger, better, brighter, bolder, and more glo"
## [1187] "rious than ever before. Thank you. God bless you a"
## [1188] "nd God bless America."
# Analiza sentymentu przy użyciu pakietu SentimentAnalysis ----
sentiment <- analyzeSentiment(text_chunks)


# odkomentuj i zobacz parametry funkcji:
?analyzeSentiment




### Słownik GI (General Inquirer) ----
#
# Słownik ogólnego zastosowania
# zawiera listę słów pozytywnych i negatywnych
# zgodnych z psychologicznym słownikiem harwardzkim Harvard IV-4
# DictionaryGI


# Wczytaj słownik GI
data(DictionaryGI)
summary(DictionaryGI)
##          Length Class  Mode     
## negative 2005   -none- character
## positive 1637   -none- character
# Konwersja ciągłych wartości sentymentu 
# na odpowiadające im wartości kierunkowe 
# zgodnie ze słownikiem GI
sentimentGI <- convertToDirection(sentiment$SentimentGI)


# Wykres skumulowanego sentymentu kierunkowego
plot(sentimentGI)

# Analiza sentymentu przy użyciu pakietu SentimentAnalysis ----
# Analiza sentymentu przy użyciu pakietu SentimentAnalysis ----
# Ten sam wykres w ggplot2:
# Konwersja do ramki danych (ggplot wizualizuje ramki danych)
df_GI <- data.frame(index = seq_along(sentimentGI), value = sentimentGI, Dictionary = "GI")

# Usunięcie wierszy, które zawierają NA
df_GI <- na.omit(df_GI)

ggplot(df_GI, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "green", alpha = 0.7) + 
  labs(title = "Skumulowany sentyment (GI)",
       x = "Sentyment",
       y = "Liczba") +
  theme_bw()

### Słownik HE (Henry’s Financial dictionary) ----
#
# zawiera listę słów pozytywnych i negatywnych
# zgodnych z finansowym słownikiem "Henry 2008"
# pierwszy, jaki powstał w wyniku analizy komunikatów prasowych 
# dotyczących zysków w branży telekomunikacyjnej i usług IT
# DictionaryHE


# Wczytaj słownik HE
data(DictionaryHE)
summary(DictionaryHE)
##          Length Class  Mode     
## negative  85    -none- character
## positive 105    -none- character
# Konwersja ciągłych wartości sentymentu 
# na odpowiadające im wartości kierunkowe 
# zgodnie ze słownikiem HE
sentimentHE <- convertToDirection(sentiment$SentimentHE)


# Wykres skumulowanego sentymentu kierunkowego
plot(sentimentHE)

# Ten sam wykres w ggplot2:
# Konwersja do ramki danych (ggplot wizualizuje ramki danych)
df_HE <- data.frame(index = seq_along(sentimentHE), value = sentimentHE, Dictionary = "HE")

# Usunięcie wierszy, które zawierają NA
df_HE <- na.omit(df_HE)

ggplot(df_HE, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.7) + 
  labs(title = "Skumulowany sentyment (HE)",
       x = "Sentyment",
       y = "Liczba") +
  theme_bw()

### Słownik LM (Loughran-McDonald Financial dictionary) ----
#
# zawiera listę słów pozytywnych i negatywnych oraz związanych z niepewnością
# zgodnych z finansowym słownikiem Loughran-McDonald
# DictionaryLM


# Wczytaj słownik LM
data(DictionaryLM)
summary(DictionaryLM)
##             Length Class  Mode     
## negative    2355   -none- character
## positive     354   -none- character
## uncertainty  297   -none- character
# Konwersja ciągłych wartości sentymentu 
# na odpowiadające im wartości kierunkowe 
# zgodnie ze słownikiem LM
sentimentLM <- convertToDirection(sentiment$SentimentLM)


# Wykres skumulowanego sentymentu kierunkowego
plot(sentimentLM)

# Ten sam wykres w ggplot2:
# Konwersja do ramki danych (ggplot wizualizuje ramki danych)
df_LM <- data.frame(index = seq_along(sentimentLM), value = sentimentLM, Dictionary = "LM")

# Usunięcie wierszy, które zawierają NA
df_LM <- na.omit(df_LM)
